steps.title: Spot the pattern in the passage
steps.content: Every trait the passage attributes to Robert pairs a strength with a restraint: “daring, without being rash; prompt, but not thoughtless; firm, without being harsh.” The passage then says he won the respect of his officers and the love of his comrades. The main idea must capture this balance and what it means for his military role.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Robert has put great effort into getting the attention of his superior officers.: the passage says he won their respect through character, not that he exerted effort to attract attention. ✗
  • Choice B — Robert is well suited to his role in the military.: his balanced traits — decisive yet disciplined, kind yet firm — are exactly what makes him effective; winning respect and love confirms the fit. ✓
  • Choice C — Robert is content with his life despite having made some mistakes.: the passage mentions no mistakes or discontent; this is invented. ✗
  • Choice D — Robert does whatever his comrades need done even if his superiors don’t order him to do so.: the passage does not describe Robert acting independently of orders; it describes his character traits and the resulting respect he earned. ✗
B is correct.
